name: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 01.06.2026

origin.filename: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 01.06.2026.pdf

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Government of India Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare
texts.text: Government of India Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Weekly report/ Minutes of the Crop Weather Watch Group (CWWG) meeting as on 01.06.2026
texts.text: Weekly report/ Minutes of the Crop Weather Watch Group (CWWG) meeting as on 01.06.2026

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1. Rabi Season harvest and Summer Season Sowing Status:
texts.text: 1. Rabi Season harvest and Summer Season Sowing Status:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  As  per  3 rd Advance  Estimates  2025-26,  the  area  sown  under  the  summer  crops  is  87.26  lakh  ha  as compared to 83.91 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year. As compared to previous year, Rice area declined by 0.65 lakh ha whereas, Maize area increased by 2.08 lakh ha, Groundnut by 1.03 lakh ha, Urad by 0.76 lakh ha and Shree Anna by 0.70 lakh ha.
texts.text:  As  per  3 rd Advance  Estimates  2025-26,  the  area  sown  under  the  summer  crops  is  87.26  lakh  ha  as compared to 83.91 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year. As compared to previous year, Rice area declined by 0.65 lakh ha whereas, Maize area increased by 2.08 lakh ha, Groundnut by 1.03 lakh ha, Urad by 0.76 lakh ha and Shree Anna by 0.70 lakh ha.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  As on 01.06.2026, rabi crops harvesting has been completed in all states.
texts.text:  As on 01.06.2026, rabi crops harvesting has been completed in all states.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Temperature:  Maximum  temperature  is  likely  to  be  below  normal  in  most of  country except  in Northeast India.
texts.text: Temperature:  Maximum  temperature  is  likely  to  be  below  normal  in  most of  country except  in Northeast India.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Rainfall  Distribution:  From  March  01-  May  31  2026,  the  All-India  cumulative  rainfall  is  1% higher than its Long Period Average (LPA), with regional variations: (i) East & Northeast India: 1%; (ii) Northwest India: (-) 6%; (iii) Central India: 17%; and South Peninsula: 0%.
texts.text: Rainfall  Distribution:  From  March  01-  May  31  2026,  the  All-India  cumulative  rainfall  is  1% higher than its Long Period Average (LPA), with regional variations: (i) East & Northeast India: 1%; (ii) Northwest India: (-) 6%; (iii) Central India: 17%; and South Peninsula: 0%.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The Western Disturbance as a cyclonic circulation over northwest Uttar Pradesh & neighbourhood between 3.1 & 7.6 km above mean sea level with a trough aloft in middle & upper tropospheric westerlies with its axis at 5.8 km above mean sea level roughly along Long 78°E to the north of Lat 20°N persists.
texts.text:  The Western Disturbance as a cyclonic circulation over northwest Uttar Pradesh & neighbourhood between 3.1 & 7.6 km above mean sea level with a trough aloft in middle & upper tropospheric westerlies with its axis at 5.8 km above mean sea level roughly along Long 78°E to the north of Lat 20°N persists.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Isolated heavy rainfall likely over Northeast India and South Peninsular India during the week.
texts.text:  Isolated heavy rainfall likely over Northeast India and South Peninsular India during the week.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Moderate  to  Severe  Thunderstorm  activity  with  squally  wind  speed  upto  90kmph  and  isolated hailstorm likely over Northwest, Central & adjoining East India on 31st May. Isolated Hailstorm activity likely over Odisha, West Madhya Pradesh and Madhya Maharashtra on 01 st June.
texts.text:  Moderate  to  Severe  Thunderstorm  activity  with  squally  wind  speed  upto  90kmph  and  isolated hailstorm likely over Northwest, Central & adjoining East India on 31st May. Isolated Hailstorm activity likely over Odisha, West Madhya Pradesh and Madhya Maharashtra on 01 st June.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Normal to above normal rainfall is expected over central and northwest India.
texts.text:  Normal to above normal rainfall is expected over central and northwest India.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Heat wave condition likely to prevail over Bihar during 04th-06th June.
texts.text:  Heat wave condition likely to prevail over Bihar during 04th-06th June.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Water  Reservoir  Status :  As  on  29  May  2026,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  166  important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 56.300 billion cubic meter, which is 30.67% of total live storage capacity (183.565 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 100.73 percent of last  year's  storage  and  119.11  percent  of  the  average  storage  of  the  last  ten  years  during  the corresponding period. The overall storage position is better than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.
texts.text: Water  Reservoir  Status :  As  on  29  May  2026,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  166  important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 56.300 billion cubic meter, which is 30.67% of total live storage capacity (183.565 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 100.73 percent of last  year's  storage  and  119.11  percent  of  the  average  storage  of  the  last  ten  years  during  the corresponding period. The overall storage position is better than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Groundwater  Levels :  In  January,  2026,  around  75.8%  of  the  country's  monitoring  stations exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper water levels of more than 20 m are observed in around 13.5% of stations of the country covering mainly the western states, especially Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.
texts.text: Groundwater  Levels :  In  January,  2026,  around  75.8%  of  the  country's  monitoring  stations exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper water levels of more than 20 m are observed in around 13.5% of stations of the country covering mainly the western states, especially Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The  deeper  groundwater  level  in  the  states  like  Western  Rajasthan,  Punjab,  Haryana  and Chandigarh  during  January,  2026  has  been  significantly  influenced  by  the  over  extraction  of groundwater compared to the recharge in the area, indicating negative water balance.
texts.text: The  deeper  groundwater  level  in  the  states  like  Western  Rajasthan,  Punjab,  Haryana  and Chandigarh  during  January,  2026  has  been  significantly  influenced  by  the  over  extraction  of groundwater compared to the recharge in the area, indicating negative water balance.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: About 70% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level in January, 2026 when compared to the decadal mean water level of 2016-2025.
texts.text: About 70% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level in January, 2026 when compared to the decadal mean water level of 2016-2025.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The  Groundwater  Drought  Indices  indicate  that  in  January,  2026,  groundwater  conditions  across India remained largely stable, with 90.30% of monitoring stations falling under normal condition in respect of Ground Water Drought. However, localized deficits were observed in several regions notably in Arunachal Pradesh, Himachal Pradesh, Meghalaya, and Nagaland.
texts.text: The  Groundwater  Drought  Indices  indicate  that  in  January,  2026,  groundwater  conditions  across India remained largely stable, with 90.30% of monitoring stations falling under normal condition in respect of Ground Water Drought. However, localized deficits were observed in several regions notably in Arunachal Pradesh, Himachal Pradesh, Meghalaya, and Nagaland.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: c) Fertilizers: The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.
texts.text: Fertilizers: The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4. Pests & Diseases : The overall incidence of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level. The monitoring of Cotton crop for Pink Ball Worm is progressing as per the protocol.
texts.text: Pests & Diseases : The overall incidence of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level. The monitoring of Cotton crop for Pink Ball Worm is progressing as per the protocol.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 5. Crops whose Mandi Wholesale prices are below MSP:
texts.text: 5. Crops whose Mandi Wholesale prices are below MSP:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: All the commodities are currently going below MSP during this week.
texts.text: All the commodities are currently going below MSP during this week.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  During the week (21 May - 27 May 2026), lower rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 3%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st March to  27  May  2026) is  higher  than LPA by 1%.
texts.text:  During the week (21 May - 27 May 2026), lower rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 3%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st March to  27  May  2026) is  higher  than LPA by 1%.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Small figures indicate actual rainfal (mm), while bold figures indicate Normal rainfall (mm).
texts.text: Small figures indicate actual rainfal (mm), while bold figures indicate Normal rainfall (mm).

texts.label: footnote
texts.orig: a) RainFall figures are based on operation data.
texts.text: a) RainFall figures are based on operation data.

texts.label: footnote
texts.orig: c) Percentage Departures of rainfall are shown in brackets.
texts.text: c) Percentage Departures of rainfall are shown in brackets.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Normal Rainfall activity likely over central and Northeast India. Below normal rainfall is likely over south peninsular India and western Himalayan region.
texts.text: Normal Rainfall activity likely over central and Northeast India. Below normal rainfall is likely over south peninsular India and western Himalayan region.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Maximum temperatures are very likely to be 5-6 Deg Celsius below normal over most parts of the country during the week except over North-Eastern States.
texts.text:  Maximum temperatures are very likely to be 5-6 Deg Celsius below normal over most parts of the country during the week except over North-Eastern States.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Low probability of Heatwave conditions likely over some parts of northwest India (Rajasthan, south Punjab, south Haryana, south Uttar Pradesh) during some days of the week.
texts.text:  Low probability of Heatwave conditions likely over some parts of northwest India (Rajasthan, south Punjab, south Haryana, south Uttar Pradesh) during some days of the week.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Above-normal (+1.6 to +3.0°C) to appreciably above-normal (+3.1 to +5.0°C)  maximum temperatures are likely over some parts of East India (Bihar, Gangetic West Bengal & Odisha). Nearnormal  weekly  maximum  temperatures  (-1.5  to  +1.5°C)  are  likely  across  the  rest  of  the  country during the week.
texts.text:  Above-normal (+1.6 to +3.0°C) to appreciably above-normal (+3.1 to +5.0°C)  maximum temperatures are likely over some parts of East India (Bihar, Gangetic West Bengal & Odisha). Nearnormal  weekly  maximum  temperatures  (-1.5  to  +1.5°C)  are  likely  across  the  rest  of  the  country during the week.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Maximum temperatures are very likely to be 4-5 Deg Celsius below normal over most parts of the country  during  week  2 except  many  parts  of  Odisha,  West  Bengal,  Bihar,  and  Arunachal Pradesh, West Rajasthan, Punjab.
texts.text:  Maximum temperatures are very likely to be 4-5 Deg Celsius below normal over most parts of the country  during  week  2 except  many  parts  of  Odisha,  West  Bengal,  Bihar,  and  Arunachal Pradesh, West Rajasthan, Punjab.

texts.text:  The  Northern  Limit  of  Monsoon  continues  to  pass  through  10°N/60°E,  9°/65°E,  8.5°N/70°E, 8°N/75°E, 7.8°N/78°E, 8°N/80°E, 10°N/80°E, 13°N/87°E, 16°N/90°E, 19°N/93°E and 22°N/97°E.

texts.text:  Conditions  are  favourable  for  further  advance  of  southwest  monsoon  into  some  more  parts  of southwest & southeast Arabian Sea, Lakshadweep Islands, some parts of Kerala & Tamil Nadu,

texts.orig:  Quantitatively, the southwest monsoon seasonal rainfall over the country as a whole is likely to be 90% of the Long Period Average (LPA) with a model error of ±4%, indicating that below normal or less rainfall is most likely.
texts.text:  Quantitatively, the southwest monsoon seasonal rainfall over the country as a whole is likely to be 90% of the Long Period Average (LPA) with a model error of ±4%, indicating that below normal or less rainfall is most likely.
